bells_01.name: Swan (Bells 1)
bells_01.body: If you play this card and lose the trick, you lead the next trick.
bells_01.details: Even if your opponent wins this trick, you get to lead the next trick instead of them.

bells_03.name: Fox (Bells 3)
bells_03.body: When you play this card, you may exchange the Decree Card with a card from your hand.
bells_03.details: You may change the trump suit by swapping a card from your hand with the face-up Decree Card. The new Decree Card determines the new trump suit.

bells_05.name: Woodcutter (Bells 5)
bells_05.body: When you play this card, draw 1 card from the draw deck. Then discard any 1 card from your hand to the bottom of the draw deck (face down).
bells_05.details: This lets you cycle through your hand, potentially finding better cards or ditching unwanted ones.

bells_07.name: Treasure (Bells 7)
bells_07.body: The winner of a trick containing this card scores 1 point for each 7 in the trick.
bells_07.details: Points from Treasure cards are scored immediately when the trick is won, in addition to end-of-round scoring.

bells_09.name: Witch (Bells 9)
bells_09.body: When determining the winner of a trick with only one 9, treat the 9 as if it were in the trump suit.
bells_09.details: A single 9 acts like a trump card. If both players play a 9, they cancel out and act as their actual suits.

bells_11.name: Monarch (Bells 11)
bells_11.body: When you lead this card, your opponent must play either the 1 of this suit or their highest-ranked card of this suit (if they have any cards of this suit).
bells_11.details: Your opponent must play their 1 (Swan) if they have it, otherwise their highest card of the led suit. This can force out high cards or deny them the Swan's ability.
